data.title: Phoenix Group Redeems $250 Million of Restricted Tier 1 Notes
data.description:
Phoenix Group said Wednesday it completed the early redemption of $250 million restricted tier 1 notes.

The British insurance company said the buyback is part of its ongoing debt redemption program, noting that it notified note holders of the plan on Jan. 2.

data.title: Strength of Australian Market Sector's Jobs Growth Allays Risk of Slowdown in Non-Market Employment, Says ANZ Research
data.description:
A potential slowdown in jobs growth in the non-market sector is no longer a threat in terms of the headline unemployment figure and the Reserve Bank of Australia's easing cycle given the recent strength of the market sector, ANZ Research said in a Thursday note.

Jobs growth in Australia's non-market sector, which comprises health care and social assistance, education and training, and public administration and safety, lagged that in the market sector in the fourth quarter of 2024.

Over the year to the fourth quarter, jobs growth was highest in accommodation and food services, while that in health care and social assistance stood around the middle, ANZ Research said.

Within health care and social assistance, jobs in the care sector fell by 51,700 in the fourth quarter.

"That the care sector can show [such] a fall in employment while the unemployment rate moves sideways, suggests the market sector was able to fill the gap created by a decline in care sector employment.

It is also possible that the increase in market sector employment in Q4 pulled workers out of the care sector," ANZ Research wrote.

data.title: Market Chatter: Taiwan's Business Climate Monitor Indicates Boom in December 2024
data.description:
Taiwan's National Development Council raised its business climate monitor to "red" for December 2024, signaling an economic boom, the Taipei Times reported on Wednesday.

The surge was driven by strong demand for electronics essential to artificial intelligence (AI) development. The composite score climbed four points from November 2024 to 38, thanks to strong technology product sales and increased pre-Lunar New Year demand, the report said.

The NDC employs a five-color system to gauge the country's economic conditions, the publication noted.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Cummins India rises on Q3 profit growth
data.description:

** Says overall sales up ~22% y/y, which largely included the sales of engines and power generators

** Adds co remains cautiously optimistic about demand outlook for the near-term

** CUMM last up 1.7%, trims YTD losses to 10.8%

data.title: Rural demand, price hikes power India consumer goods sector growth, NielsenIQ says
data.description:
Solid demand in rural areas, as well as higher prices of staples including edible oil and wheat flour, helped the consumer goods sector report a 10.6% sales growth in the December quarter, market researcher NielsenIQ said on Thursday.

India's rural areas - which account for just over a third of consumer goods sales - have proven a bright spot for an industry struggling with an inflation-led spending slowdown in large cities.

"Rural markets (continued) to lead the charge, outpacing urban consumption (during the December quarter)," Roosevelt Dsouza, head of customer success for consumer goods at NielsenIQ, said in a statement.

Sales volume jumped 9.9% in rural areas in the December quarter, up from 5.7% in the previous quarter - double the 5% increase in urban centers, NielsenIQ said. But it added urban pockets also improved from the September quarter's 2.6% growth.

Rural areas have outperformed urban locations for the last four quarters, benefiting from income support schemes rolled out by several Indian states, while slowing salary increases in cities have squeezed consumer spending.

In the October-December quarter, overall volume rose by 7.1% - the highest in over a year - driven by demand for laundry products and edible oil, even as prices rose by 3.3%, according to NielsenIQ.

Dabur India and Hindustan Unilever reported a higher December-quarter profit on recovering rural demand.

However, large consumer goods makers, with topline exceeding 50 billion rupees ($571.2 million) are also facing stiff competition from smaller rivals, whose sales increased roughly twice as fast during the festive quarter, NielsenIQ said.

Consumer goods makers have also raised product prices to counter price increases in commodities such as copra and cocoa, with cooking oil maker Adani Wilmar and Hindustan Unilever warning of further hikes.

Indians also preferred smaller product packs during the quarter, NielsenIQ said, echoing comments from Hindustan Unilever.

($1 = 87.5400 Indian rupees)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Thursday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Trump and Musk's Actions Amount to Significant Pullback on Financial Regulation

Agencies overseeing consumer financial affairs, wealth managers, and commodity traders all seem to be scaling back oversight activity.

Fed's Barkin Says Economy Is Fine. Government Policy Is a Wild Card.

It's too early to determine the impact of tariffs and other Trump policies on the economy, but the uncertainty is having an effect, the Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond president said.

Fannie, Freddie Privatization, Cost-Cutting and New Name Are Priorities for HUD Chief

Scott Turner says he plans to quickly launch a review to root out inefficiencies at the agency.

Hillenbrand Sells Majority Stake in Milacron to Bain Capital Affiliate

The provider of highly-engineered processing equipment and solutions will sell a 51% stake in the injection molding and extrusion business for $287 million.

Banks Sell $5.5 Billion of X Loans After Investor Interest Surges

X CEO tells investors that the financial health of social-media company is improving.

Allstate Expects California Wildfires to Cost $1.1 Billion

In the fourth quarter, catastrophe losses took $315 million out of Allstate's homeowners-insurance business, up from $21 million the year before.

Ares Management Expands Leadership After Notching a Record Year

The credit specialist elevated two firm veterans to newly created co-president roles to support future growth.

SEC Ousts Top Litigator Who Battled With Crypto Giants

The agency, seeking to make peace with the crypto market, reassigned its head of litigation to a job in its information-technology office.

Options Traders Bet the Rally in Big Tech Stocks Has More Room to Run

They are scooping up contracts that would pay out if stocks like Apple and Amazon race higher in the next few weeks.

Crypto Industry Looks to New SEC Task Force for Quick Action in Its Favor

Industry groups have a wish list they say could bring more regulatory clarity around cryptocurrencies and allow for growth.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Thursday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Oil prices drop on rise in U.S. supplies as tariffs threaten to dent Chinese demand

Oil futures on Wednesday marked their lowest settlement prices of the year, after official U.S. data showed a weekly climb of nearly 9 million barrels in U.S. crude inventories and as the U.S.-China trade battle threatened to dent demand.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories increased more than expected as production picked up, while the build in gasoline stocks extended to almost three months.

Orsted Cuts 2030 Investment Program by 25%

The Danish group energy group said that 2024 Ebitda was in line with expectations, but acknowledged it experienced challenges, especially related to its U.S. offshore wind business.

Venezuela's Citgo Is Proving a Tough Sell for Bidders

The coming auction for Venezuela's Citgo Petroleum could leave its buyer on the hook to other creditors of the South American country, a stumbling block for the court-ordered sale.

Energy Is More Exposed Than Ever to a Trade War

U.S. energy is a much bigger industry than it was in President Donald Trump's first term.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Read about U.S. oil inventories, Equinor, TotalEnergies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

OMV's Net Profit Rises Despite Weak Refining Margins

The oil-and-gas company's refining margin fell to $5.9 a barrel, compared with $9.9 a barrel a year ago.

Oil Rises as Trump's Tariffs Raise Supply Fears

Prices jumped on concerns that U.S. tariffs against its major trade partners could disrupt supplies and raise prices at the pump.

Exxon Enters the Electricity Business, Takes a Swipe at Nuclear

Exxon says it can compete with nuclear, because the gas power plants it intends to build would capture carbon emissions.

U.S. Frackers and Saudi Officials Tell Trump They Won't Drill More

The president says lower prices could solve a range of problems, but finds early resistance in the oil market.

RAK Properties' FY24 Net Profit, Revenue Climb
data.description:
RAK Properties (ADX:RAKPROP) said Wednesday that its net profit and revenue for full-year 2024 jumped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 280.9 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 201.8 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.111 dirham from 0.097 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed property developer's revenue was 1.41 billion dirhams, compared with 1 billion dirhams earlier.

data.title: Hawkish BOJ Board Member Calls for Potentially Faster Rate Hikes — Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

A hawkish member of the Bank of Japan's policy board on Thursday called for potentially faster interest-rate increases, sending the yen to its strongest level against the dollar in eight weeks.

In a speech to business leaders in Nagano prefecture in central Japan, Naoki Tamura said that the central bank should raise rates to 1% or higher in the fiscal half starting in October. That level is likely consistent with a neutral rate setting that is neither restrictive nor stimulating for the economy, he added.

"Raising short-term interest rates to at least around 1% in the latter half of fiscal 2025 is necessary to reduce upside risk to prices and achieve the price stability target in a sustainable and stable manner," he said.

Tamura's comments suggest that the central bank may tighten monetary policy more quickly than some economists and investors have been expecting. Consensus views are for one or two more 25-basis-point hikes in the next fiscal year, but the board member suggested that the policy rate could go beyond 1%.

After his speech, the yen went to around 151.80 against the dollar, the strongest level since Dec. 11.

Many economists expect the BOJ to raise the policy rate again later this year, but some have cautioned that the next move requires careful consideration as rates approach levels Japan hasn't experienced in 30 years.

Tamura is a former executive at Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group and considered hawkish. He proposed a rate increase in December, citing increased risks of higher consumer prices. The proposal was defeated by a majority vote, but the policy board then decided to lift interest rates to 0.5% from 0.25% at the bank's January meeting.

Tamura's comments could well be testing the waters, seeing how markets react to the idea of rate hikes at least every six months, said Mizuho Securities economist Yusuke Matsuo.

The central bank has been making efforts to improve its communication to markets, with the goal of minimizing disruptive reactions to its policy moves.

That said, Matsuo thinks the BOJ may have to stop once rates reach 0.75% because he expects inflation to slow.

Tamura seems to have a different outlook, saying in the speech that the bank should be able to achieve its goal of creating sustainable, stable 2% inflation in the second half of fiscal 2025 when wage trends among small companies become clearer.

Growth and mild inflation backed by higher wages is a central goal for the BOJ.

Tamura also voiced concern that inflation--which has been above 2% for nearly three years--and a recent spike in rice prices may hurt consumer sentiment. Consumer inflation picked up to 3.6% on year in December, compared with 2.9% in November.

Even if the BOJ raises interest rates to 0.75%, monetary conditions will remain easy as real interest rates will stay negative, the board member said.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Paycor HCM Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Paycor HCM Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 14 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 11 cents. The mean expectation of seventeen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 12 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 11 cents to 13 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 13.1% to $180.44 million from a year ago; analysts expected $177.16 million.

  • Paycor HCM Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 1 cent​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $2.05 million.

  • Paycor HCM Inc shares had risen by 19.2% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 13 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the software peer group is also "hold"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Paycor HCM Inc is $22.50

data.title: India's Sula Vineyards falls on lower Q3 profit
data.description:
** Shares of Indian winemaker Sula Vineyards down ~4% at 350.3 rupees

** Co reports a 35% fall in Q3 profit on lower demand amid a slowdown in urban consumption and election-related disruptions in Maharashtra state, a key market for co

** Total revenue down 0.4% in Q3, while total expenses up 11.4%

** Sula gets about 90% of its revenue from urban markets

** Sula's profit margin contracts to 24.8% from 33.5% last year

** Including session's decline, shares have fallen 45.5% y/y

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on Feb 6
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira traded at 35.9390 against the U.S. dollar at 0452 GMT, after closing at 35.8925 on Wednesday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.89% lower at 9,719.81 points on Wednesday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asia shares rose on Thursday, tracking gains on Wall Street following a see-saw session, while U.S. Treasury yields came under pressure after mixed economic data.

EARTHQUAKE ANNIVERSARY

Turkey marks the two-year anniversary of its worst natural disaster: the 7.8-magnitude earthquake that killed some 53,000 people in the country and another 6,000 in neighbouring Syria on a winter night in 2023.

President Tayyip Erdogan, main opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel, and several ministers, including those responsible for labor, culture, energy, trade, agriculture, and industry, will attend ceremonies across the earthquake region.

ISTANBUL MAYOR

Turkish prosecutors have issued a third indictment against Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu - a potential challenger to President Tayyip Erdogan - for remarks criticising the city's prosecutor, according to the indictment and local media.

ISLAMIC STATE

Turkey, Iraq, Syria and Jordan will take steps toward jointly combating Islamic State in the region and they aim to hold a first meeting on the issue in Jordan, Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said on Wednesday.

PARLIAMENT

Turkish Parliament convenes to discuss items on its agenda (1100).

POPULATION STATISTICS

Turkish Statistical Institute announces 2024 address-based population registration system results (0700).

BANKING DATA

Turkish Central Bank releases weekly money and banking data, foreign exchange reserves, and securities movements (1130).

data.title: Whitbread Prices Offer of GBP400 Million Guaranteed Notes
data.description:
Whitbread priced an offering of 400 million pounds sterling of 5.50% guaranteed notes due 2032, according to a Wednesday filing.

The notes are expected to be issued on Feb. 12. The British hotel and restaurant company earmarked proceeds from the offering for general corporate expenditures, including debt refinancing.

data.title: Market Chatter: Singapore's Real Estate Transaction Volumes Rebound in 2024 with 28% Increase
data.description:
Singapore's real estate transaction volumes increased by 28% to SG$28.6 billion in 2024 following a 30.3% decline in 2023, according to a report by Singapore Business Review on Wednesday.

The increase in transaction volume was mostly due to a surge in transactions within the industrial and logistic sectors, the report added.

According to CBRE's 2025 Singapore Real Estate Market Outlook report, retail prime rents are expected to increase by 2% to 3% in 2025, led by Orchard Road and City Hall/Marina Centre.

Residential prices are expected to rise by 3% to 6% with developers to launch about 14,000 units during the year, the report added.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Lindian Resources Gives $1.3 Million Site Access Road Contract for Malawian Rare Earths Project; Shares Down 5%
data.description:
Lindian Resources gave a $1.3 million site access road contract to European construction group Mota-Engil for its Kangankunde rare earths project in Malawi, the company said in a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The contract includes the intersection of the access road with the main M1 highway, 5 kilometers of access road works, and civil works around the mining lease access area with an associated security area, added the filing.

Company shares dropped almost 5% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Shanghai Junshi Biosciences Gets Clinical Trial Approval for Anti-tumor Drug; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Shanghai Junshi Biosciences obtained approval from China's drug administrator to conduct clinical trials for its AWT020 sterile powder for injection.

AWT020 will be carried out as a single drug and in combination with other treatments in patients with advanced malignant tumors, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the pharmaceutical company rose 3% in recent trade on the Shanghai bourse.
